{"key_event":"Institutionalized dictatorship amid revived opposition","reasoning":"The 1980 Constitution, CNI repression, emergency powers, and military safeguards kept the Chilean state overwhelmingly powerful, though crisis, protests, and controlled electoral opening slightly dented its aura and discretion. Civil society remained constrained and often repressed, but Church-protected networks, mass protest days, opposition blocs, and party legalization rebuilt enough organization for moderate, periodic mobilization.","society_change":1.8,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":9.0}
